name: compile
description: Translate a RiscvBtor2Spec into a BTOR2 reasoning artifact and return its content-addressed id. Determinism is guaranteed for a fixed (spec, source, schema_version) tuple. Run this after introspect confirms the spec is well-formed. A v1.1.0 spec carrying BranchPin assumptions and/or CycleInvariant with dual_role=true is supported and produces a layered artifact whose volatile layer encodes the pinned-prefix or dual-role bad clauses.

spec.description: A complete RiscvBtor2Spec object. Must satisfy the schema in gurdy/pairs/riscv_btor2/spec.py and validate_riscv_btor2_spec. Pair field must be "riscv-btor2". Schema version 1.1.0 introduces BranchPin assumptions and CycleInvariant.dual_role.

name: dispatch
description: Run the named solver against a previously-compiled artifact and return its raw verdict. The directive picks the engine, bound, timeout, havoc set, and engine-specific options.

artifact_id.description: The id returned by a previous compile call.

directive.description: AnalysisDirective. Required: engine. Optional: bound (int), timeout (seconds, float), havoc_registers (array of register numbers 0..31), extra_options (object).

name: lift
description: Translate a raw solver result back to source-level steps. For reachable verdicts, returns a WitnessTrace with per-cycle (pc, mnemonic, regs); for proved verdicts (Pono / Spacer), returns a lifted invariant. Read off the witness JSON's bad_pc, anchor_step, and final_regs from the returned trace.

raw_result.description: A RawSolverResult object as returned by dispatch.

name: introspect
description: Run the pair's structural validator on a spec without compiling. Returns an array of Diagnostic objects (empty when the spec is well-formed). Use before compile to catch schema mismatches cheaply.

name: simulate
description: Run the pair's source interpreter on a concrete or partial input binding for up to max_steps steps. With record_shadow=true (the v1.1.0 question-compiler hook, SCHEMA.md §14.6), the trace's final_state.shadow exposes branch_events, memory_events, and free_fields — the building blocks for BranchPin assumptions and propose-and-check iteration. Without record_shadow, free binding cells ("FREE" sentinels) are rejected.

spec.description: A complete RiscvBtor2Spec — the simulator uses spec.binary and any EntryAssumption-derived excluded PC ranges.

binding.description: RiscvInputBinding. Keys: register_init (object {regnum_str: uint64 | "Free"}), memory_init (object {addr_str_or_int: uint8 | "Free"}), havoc_per_step (array of per-step register overrides, same shape as register_init). All keys optional; an empty binding runs the program at its natural entry state. A "Free" cell requires record_shadow=true.

max_steps.description: Maximum number of source-level instructions to execute before halting the trace.

record_shadow.description: When true, accepts "FREE" cells in the binding (concretized to 0) and records per-step branch/memory events on final_state.shadow.
